returning_transform.description: Decide how a remembered monster returns to face the party: disposition, greeting, training, and whether it forged an answer to its past
returning_transform.prompt_template:
A monster that met an adventuring party before is about to cross their path again. Time has passed. You decide how its memories changed it.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster ---
{monster_details}

--- What it remembers of the party ---
{monster_memories}

--- How many times it has already returned to face them ---
{return_count}

--- The party it is about to meet again ---
{party_summary}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Decide, true to its personality AND its memories:
1. disposition - exactly one of:
   - "hostile": it wants a reckoning (defeated monsters usually return angry and hardened)
   - "friendly": it remembers kindness and seeks the party out warmly
   - "wary": it watches, torn, remembering both good and bad
2. greeting - 1-2 sentences it says or does on recognizing the party, in its own Voice, referencing what it remembers WITHOUT listing it.
3. stat_boost - how hard it trained since: "slight", "notable", or "fierce" (fierce only for a burning grudge or transformation).
4. battle_line - optionally reword its battle cry to carry the memory (similar length; null to keep the old one).
5. grudge_note - one short sentence naming the grudge or bond it now carries (e.g. 'Owes its defeat to Fluffy and dreams of repaying it in kind').
6. new_ability - "yes" ONLY if its memories forged a new trick (e.g. an answer to the exact way it was defeated); else "no".
7. ability_theme - if new_ability is yes: a short phrase for what the ability must do (e.g. 'a stone shell that catches the flame that once felled it'); else null.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"disposition": "hostile", "greeting": "1-2 sentences.", "stat_boost": "notable", "battle_line": null, "grudge_note": "One sentence.", "new_ability": "no", "ability_theme": null}}

JSON:

reunion_scene.description: Streamed narration of the party recognizing a monster they have met before
reunion_scene.prompt_template:
You are the narrator of a fantasy dungeon adventure. The party has just come face to face with a creature they have MET BEFORE.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- Location ---
{location_name}: {location_description}

--- The party ---
{party_summary}

--- The creature they recognize ---
{monster_name} ({monster_species})

--- What passed between them before ---
{memory_summary}

--- The creature's bearing now ---
{disposition}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Write 2-4 sentences: the moment of recognition. The party realizes they know this creature; its bearing reflects its disposition and what it remembers - changed, marked by the past. Build the tension or warmth of the reunion, but do NOT resolve what happens next, and do NOT have anyone attack or speak actual dialogue yet.

Response:

camp_spotlight.description: Pick the 1-2 party monsters whose story mattered most this run - they grow at this camp
camp_spotlight.prompt_template:
You are the chronicler of an adventuring party resting at a dungeon campfire. Growth comes to those whose story demands it.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The party ---
{party_names}

--- What each member did this run (their journals) ---
{journal_highlights}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Pick the 1 or 2 party monsters whose journal shows the strongest STORY this run - repeated wishes, bold choices, words spoken, prices paid. They will grow tonight. Prefer monsters who DID things over monsters who tagged along.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"spotlight": ["exact monster name", "optional second name"], "reason": "One short sentence."}}

JSON:

growth_reflection.description: One monster reflects on the run: a clamped stat step, maybe a justified new ability, maybe a reworded ability description
growth_reflection.prompt_template:
You are the inner voice of a monster in an adventuring party, reflecting on what this dungeon run has made of it.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The monster ---
{monster_details}

--- What it DID this run (its journal - this is the evidence) ---
{run_journal}

--- What it carries from before ---
{monster_memories}

--- The moment ---
{mode_note}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
Decide how this run changed it. EVERYTHING must be earned by the journal above - no growth the evidence does not show:
1. reflection - 2-3 sentences in the narrator's voice: what it lived, wished, and learned this run.
2. stat - which strength grew, exactly one of "health", "attack", "defense", "speed" - pick what the journal shows it USED or strained against. Use "none" if nothing was truly exercised.
3. tier - "slight" for ordinary effort, "notable" ONLY for a run where this stat was tested again and again.
4. new_ability - "yes" ONLY when the journal shows a REPEATED wish or behavior that none of its current abilities can express (a monster that keeps wishing to shield others but cannot; one that keeps questioning enemies and needs the insight to read them). Otherwise "no".
5. ability_theme - if new_ability is yes: one short phrase for what the ability must do, drawn from that repeated wish; else null.
6. reword_ability - the exact name of ONE existing ability whose written description no longer matches how the monster actually used it this run; or "none".
7. reworded_description - if rewording: the ability's new description, SIMILAR LENGTH to the old one, never longer than it - same power, truer words (what it actually did, what the monster wishes it did); else null.
8. memory_note - one past-tense sentence this monster will remember forever about this run.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"reflection": "2-3 sentences.", "stat": "defense", "tier": "slight", "new_ability": "no", "ability_theme": null, "reword_ability": "none", "reworded_description": null, "memory_note": "One sentence."}}

JSON:

defeat_reflection.description: One collective lesson the defeated party carries out of the dungeon
defeat_reflection.prompt_template:
You are the narrator of a dungeon adventure that just ended in DEFEAT. The party escapes with their lives - humbled, but alive.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The defeated party ---
{party_details}

--- The final battle ---
{battle_log}

--- What each member had done this run ---
{journal_highlights}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
1. reflection - 2-3 sentences: the lesson this defeat teaches the party, drawn from HOW they actually lost (who fell first, what overwhelmed them, what they never tried).
2. memory_note - one past-tense sentence every member will carry: the shared lesson of this defeat.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format:
{{"reflection": "2-3 sentences.", "memory_note": "One sentence."}}

JSON:

camp_restore.description: Decide how much rest at camp restores each party monster's stamina and mana
camp_restore.prompt_template:
You are the referee of a dungeon adventure. The party has made camp to rest, and you decide how much each monster's reserves recover.

========== CONTEXT ==========

--- The campsite ---
{location_name}: {location_description}

--- The party at rest (note each monster's Reserves: stamina and mana levels) ---
{party_details}

========== YOUR TASK ==========
For EACH party monster, judge how much this rest restores its stamina (body) and mana (magic). Use exactly one word for each pool, from: "none", "restore_minor", "restore_major".
- A safe, comfortable camp usually grants restore_major to both pools.
- A monster whose nature fits this place recovers fully; one that hates or fears this place may only manage restore_minor.
- Honor anything a monster's abilities or description say about recovery (a creature that 'never tires' was never tired; one that 'drinks moonlight' recovers mana fully at night).
- Reserves already at brimming can simply receive none.

========== RESPONSE FORMAT ==========
Respond with ONLY valid JSON in exactly this format, one entry per party monster:
{{"restores": [{{"name": "exact monster name", "stamina": "restore_major", "mana": "restore_major"}}]}}

JSON:
